meta_title: Impact Windows in South Broward, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Protect your South Broward home with hurricane-rated impact windows. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs ESW & CWI windows—licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Get a free quote today!
h1: South Broward's Trusted Impact Window Experts — Built Tough for Florida's Most Punishing Storms
intro:
South Broward is one of the most hardworking communities in all of Broward County — a neighborhood where residents take pride in their homes, look out for their neighbors, and understand that protecting what they've built is not a luxury, it's a necessity. With average home values around $290,000 and a housing stock where more than half of all properties were constructed before 1994, the need for modern storm protection has never been more urgent. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is proud to serve homeowners throughout South Broward with professionally installed, hurricane-rated impact windows designed to stand up to the brutal realities of South Florida weather.

Located approximately five miles from the Atlantic Coast, South Broward sits close enough to the shoreline to feel the full brunt of tropical systems and far enough inland that homeowners sometimes underestimate the wind speeds their homes must endure. That's a dangerous assumption. South Broward lies within a 170 mph wind zone designation, which means the structures here — particularly older homes near South Broward Park and throughout the surrounding residential blocks — are expected to withstand some of the most powerful wind loads in the continental United States. Single-pane aluminum windows, outdated jalousie frames, and even basic double-pane glass simply cannot hold up to those forces. Impact windows can.

Neighboring cities like Hollywood, West Park, and Pembroke Park have seen a significant surge in impact window upgrades over the past decade, and South Broward homeowners are increasingly following suit — not just for storm protection, but for the year-round benefits that come with it. Lower energy bills, reduced outside noise, enhanced home security, and meaningful insurance savings are just some of the reasons that more and more families in the 33023 ZIP code are choosing to invest in impact-resistant windows. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, known throughout South Florida as The Kings of Service, makes that investment accessible with $0-down financing options starting at just $87 per month.

section_why.h2: Why South Broward Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we don't subcontract. Every single installation we perform — whether it's two windows or twenty — is handled entirely by our own trained, full-time crew. That matters enormously in a community like South Broward, where older homes often present unique installation challenges: irregular frame openings, aging stucco substrates, and window configurations that require careful measurement and expert craftsmanship. Our team brings over 60 years of combined experience to every project, and that depth of knowledge shows in the quality of our finished work.

We've completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, which means we've seen virtually every scenario a South Florida home can present. We understand the specific permitting requirements of the Broward County Building Division, we know how to coordinate inspections efficiently so your project doesn't drag on for weeks, and we know how to treat our customers the way they deserve to be treated — with honesty, transparency, and genuine respect. That's not just a tagline. Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and our hundreds of satisfied customers across the region are proof of it.

For homeowners in the lower-middle income tier that South Broward represents, trust and value go hand in hand. You want to know that you're getting a product that will truly protect your family, installed by people who know what they're doing, at a price point that doesn't require you to drain your savings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing delivers on all three. Our financing options — including $0 down and payments starting at $87 per month — mean that a family in the 33023 ZIP code can start protecting their home today without waiting until they've saved up a lump sum. And because our impact windows immediately begin generating energy savings and insurance discounts, many of our customers find that the monthly payment is partially offset from day one.

When you call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not talking to a call center or a middleman. You're talking to people who live and work in South Florida, who understand what a Category 3 landfall feels like from the inside, and who are personally invested in making sure your home is ready for whatever the Atlantic hurricane season delivers next.

section_history.h2: South Broward's Storm Legacy: Why the Threat Is Real and the Stakes Are High
section_history.body:
South Broward has been tested by nine named storms since 1990, and the scars of that history are still visible — sometimes literally — in the older homes throughout the community. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 reshaped the entire conversation about residential construction in South Florida. Though Andrew's most catastrophic damage was concentrated in Miami-Dade County to the south, Broward County sustained significant wind damage as well, and the event triggered a sweeping overhaul of the Florida Building Code that still governs what materials and methods are acceptable in new and renovated construction today. Homes built before 1994 — which account for more than 55% of South Broward's housing stock — were constructed under the pre-Andrew code standards, meaning they may be fundamentally less storm-resistant than what the current science and engineering demand.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered a particularly sobering reminder to South Broward residents. Wilma made landfall on Florida's southwest coast but traversed the entire peninsula at rapid speed, still packing hurricane-force winds when it swept through Broward County. The storm's unusually wide wind field and fast forward motion meant that gusts were sustained across the region for several hours, and homes with conventional windows suffered broken glass, water intrusion, and structural damage at alarming rates. Then Hurricane Irma in 2017 brought another round of strong winds, downed trees, and widespread power outages to the area, reinforcing what many South Broward homeowners already suspected: this community sits in harm's way on a recurring basis.

Impact windows are designed specifically to absorb the kind of punishment these storms deliver. Laminated glass layers hold together even when struck by debris traveling at extreme velocities, preventing the sudden pressure changes inside a home that can cause catastrophic structural failure. For South Broward families who have lived through even one major storm with inadequate window protection, upgrading to impact-rated windows isn't a question of if — it's a question of when.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Compliance and Permit Requirements for South Broward Impact Windows
section_code.body:
South Broward falls under the jurisdiction of the Broward County Building Division, which enforces the Florida Building Code — one of the most rigorous residential construction codes in the entire country. While South Broward is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which applies primarily to Miami-Dade County and certain coastal sections of Broward, that does not mean standard windows are acceptable. On the contrary, Florida Building Code requirements still mandate hurricane-rated products for all window installations in this area, and any window that does not meet the applicable impact or pressure standards simply cannot be legally installed.

All impact windows installed in South Broward must carry a valid Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or product approval demonstrating compliance with the required design pressure ratings for the 170 mph wind zone. These ratings account for both positive and negative pressure loads — meaning the windows must resist not only direct wind force pushing inward, but also the powerful suction forces that can pull outward during a storm. Every window Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs comes with the proper documentation, and all of our installations are permitted through the Broward County Building Division before work begins. We handle the permit application process on behalf of our customers, coordinate all required inspections, and ensure that the completed installation meets or exceeds every applicable code requirement.

For homeowners in pre-1994 homes — a majority in South Broward — obtaining a permit for window replacement may also trigger a review of the existing rough opening and framing conditions. Our experienced installation crews know how to navigate these scenarios efficiently, making any necessary modifications to bring the installation into full compliance without unnecessary delays or cost overruns. Working with a licensed, code-savvy contractor like Premier Impact Windows & Roofing protects you not only from storm damage but also from the legal and financial complications of unpermitted work.

section_products.h2: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI Impact Windows: Built for South Broward's Wind Zone
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading brands that we've selected specifically for their proven performance in South Florida's demanding climate: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both product lines are engineered to meet and exceed the requirements for 170 mph wind zones, making them ideal solutions for South Broward homes in the 33023 ZIP code. Whether you're replacing a single problem window or upgrading every opening in a pre-1994 concrete block home, these products deliver the performance, durability, and aesthetics that South Broward homeowners deserve.

ESW impact windows feature heavy-duty multi-chamber frames constructed from impact-resistant materials that resist warping, corrosion, and UV degradation — all critical considerations in South Florida's humid, salt-influenced environment even five miles from the coast. The laminated glass units use a specialized interlayer that bonds the glass layers together, so even if the outer pane is struck and cracked by windborne debris, the window holds its integrity and prevents wind, rain, and pressure changes from entering your home. Low-E coatings on ESW glass units significantly reduce solar heat gain, keeping interiors cooler and reducing the burden on air conditioning systems during South Florida's long, intense summers.

CWI impact windows offer a complementary lineup that includes a wide range of styles — single-hung, double-hung, casement, picture, and sliding configurations — allowing homeowners to achieve the look they want without sacrificing protection. CWI's frames are engineered for tight, weather-resistant seals that minimize air infiltration, improve energy efficiency, and reduce the dust and allergen penetration that plagues many older South Broward homes. Together, ESW and CWI give our customers access to a comprehensive selection of certified, high-performance impact windows that fit virtually any home style, budget, and architectural requirement found throughout the South Broward community.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Windows Cost in South Broward — And How Do You Make Them Affordable?
section_cost.body:
For a home in South Broward with an average value around $290,000, a full impact window replacement project typically represents one of the most valuable home improvement investments a homeowner can make. The actual cost varies based on the number of windows, their sizes, the specific product line selected, and any structural modifications required — but Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's transparent, no-surprise quoting process means you'll know exactly what you're paying before any work begins. Our team provides free, detailed estimates with no pressure and no obligation.

We understand that South Broward is a community where budgets are real and financial decisions are made carefully. That's exactly why we've built flexible financing options into our business model from the ground up. Qualified homeowners can start their impact window project with $0 down and payments beginning at just $87 per month. These financing options make it possible to upgrade your home's storm protection immediately, rather than waiting years to save the full amount out of pocket — a delay that could prove costly if a major storm passes through in the meantime. The financing terms are competitive, the application process is straightforward, and our team will walk you through your options clearly.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact windows in South Broward pay dividends in multiple ways simultaneously. Energy savings from Low-E glass and improved air sealing can meaningfully reduce monthly utility bills, particularly during the June-through-September cooling season. Insurance premium reductions — often in the range of 15% to 45% on the wind portion of a homeowner's policy — begin immediately after installation and wind mitigation inspection. And the windows themselves add appraised value to the property, which is significant in a market where home values in the 33023 area have been steadily appreciating. Many South Broward homeowners find that their monthly financing payment is partially or fully offset by the combined savings on energy and insurance.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for South Broward Homeowners Who Upgrade to Impact Windows
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for installing impact windows in South Broward is the direct, measurable impact on homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's property insurance market has been under significant stress in recent years, with carriers raising rates, reducing coverage, or exiting the state altogether. In this environment, any action that reduces your risk profile — and therefore your premium — carries enormous value. Impact windows are among the most recognized and rewarded upgrades in the eyes of Florida's insurance carriers.

After your impact windows are installed and permitted, you'll want to schedule a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector. This inspection documents the storm-protective features of your home — including your new impact windows — using a standardized form that is submitted directly to your insurance carrier. The results can qualify you for significant credits on the wind portion of your premium, which in Broward County represents a substantial share of total annual costs. Depending on your current coverage and carrier, South Broward homeowners frequently report premium reductions of 15% to 45% after completing a wind mitigation inspection following impact window installation. Over the life of the windows, these savings can amount to thousands of dollars — often recovering a significant portion of the original installation cost.

Major carriers serving the South Broward market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others — all recognize impact window installations as qualifying improvements under their wind mitigation discount programs. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides customers with all of the product documentation, NOA paperwork, and permit records needed to support a successful wind mitigation inspection and insurance credit application. We want to make sure that every South Broward homeowner we serve captures every dollar of savings they've earned by making this smart, protective investment.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in South Broward if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. South Broward's location approximately five miles from the Atlantic Coast does not reduce your storm exposure to a safe level. The area sits in a 170 mph wind zone designation, meaning your home is engineered to withstand extremely powerful wind forces. Windborne debris — tiles, branches, signage — travels with deadly force during a tropical storm or hurricane regardless of your distance from the shoreline. Impact windows protect your home from debris penetration and the catastrophic pressure changes that follow a window breach.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost in South Broward, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: The total cost of impact window installation in South Broward depends on the number, size, and style of windows being replaced. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides free, no-obligation estimates tailored to your specific home. For qualified homeowners, we offer $0 down financing with monthly payments starting at just $87 per month. Combined with insurance savings and energy bill reductions that begin immediately after installation, many customers find the net monthly cost is lower than they expected from day one.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact window installation in South Broward, and who handles them?
faqs.a: All impact window replacements in South Broward require a permit issued by the Broward County Building Division. The permit process ensures that the products installed meet Florida Building Code requirements for the 170 mph wind zone, and a final inspection is required upon completion. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit application and inspection coordination process on your behalf. You don't need to navigate the permitting system yourself — we manage it from start to finish as part of every installation.

faqs.q: How long does an impact window installation take for a typical South Broward home?
faqs.a: For a typical South Broward single-family home requiring 10 to 15 windows, installation is generally completed in one to two days once the products arrive. Lead times from order to installation vary based on product availability and scheduling, but Premier Impact Windows & Roofing works to keep the process as efficient as possible. Because we use our own in-house installation crew rather than subcontractors, we have direct control over scheduling and quality. We'll give you a clear timeline during your initial consultation.

faqs.q: Will impact windows lower my homeowner's insurance in South Broward?
faqs.a: Yes — and the savings can be significant. After your impact windows are installed and permitted, a licensed wind mitigation inspector will document your home's upgraded storm protection using a standardized Florida form submitted to your insurance carrier. South Broward homeowners commonly receive wind insurance premium reductions of 15% to 45% as a result. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all the product documentation and permit records you need to support a successful wind mitigation inspection and insurance discount application.
